title: Primes and Queries
description:
You are given a prime numberô $P$.

Let's defineô $V(x)$ô as the degree ofô $P$ô in the prime factorization ofô $x$. To be clearer, ifô $V(x)=y$ô thenô $x$ô is divisible byô $P^y$, but not divisible byô $P^{y+1}$. Also we define $V(0)=0$.

For example, whenô $P=3$, andô $x=45$, sinceô $45=5 \cdot 3^2$, thereforeô $V(45)=2$.

You are also given an arrayô $A$ô withô $N$ô elements. You need to processô $Q$ô queries ofô $2$ô types on this array:

  • typeô $1$ô query:ô 1ô posô valô - assign a value $val$ô to the element atô $pos$, i.e.ô $A_{pos} := val$
  • typeô $2$ô query:ô 2ô S L Rô - printô $\displaystyle\sum_{i=L}^{R}{V(A_i^S - (A_i \bmod P)^S)}$.
input:
The first line of the input gives the number of test cases,ô $T$.ô $T$ô test cases follow.

The first line of each test case containsô $3$ô space separated positive integersô $N$,ô $Q$ô andô $P$ô - the number of elements in the array, the number of queries and a prime number.

The next line containsô $N$ô positive integersô $A_1$, $A_2$, $\cdots$, $A_N$ô representing elements of arrayô $A$.

Each of the nextô $Q$ô lines describes a query, and contains either

  • $3$ô space separated positive integers:ô 1 posô val
  • or $4$ space separated positive integers:ô 2ô Sô Lô R
output: For each test case, output one line containingô Case #x:ô y, whereô $x$ô is the test case number (starting from 1) andô $y$ô is a list of the answers for each query of typeô $2$.
hint:
In Sample Case #1

The first query is a query of type $2$, whereô $S=3$, $L=3$, $R=4$. Let's calculate the result for this query:

$i=3$, $V(62^3 - (62 \bmod 2)^3)=3$

$i=4$, $V(67^3 - (67 \bmod 2)^3)=1$

$\displaystyle\sum_{i=3}^{4}{V(A_i^3 - (A_i \bmod P)^3)} = 3+1 = 4$

The second query is of typeô $1$, where we need to assignô $69$ô toô $A_1$, so our arrayô $A$ô now becomes:ô 69 94 62 67 91.
source: Contest, Google, Kick Start, Google Kick Start 2021, Round D
